title: Why didnt NERV give the Evas S2 Engines
display_title: Why didnt NERV give the Evas S2 Engines
wikitext:
[]: Why didn't NERV give the Evas S2 Engines?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Nov 11, 2009 8:18 pm That would have fixed every problem with the cables, and it would have saved their asses loads of times. Eva-01 is probably the only one that shouldn't have one, but the others... Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Nov 11, 2009 8:21 pm ... They didn't have an S2 Engine to give them. Not until the MP Evangelions came out. Unit-04 was supposed to be the first Evangelion with a working S2 Engine installed, but as we know in the activation test it disappeared. Naturally, the next working engines would go with the following Evas in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Nov 11, 2009 8:25 pm Sachi wrote: ... They didn't have an S2 Engine to give them. Not until the MP Evangelions came out. Unit-04 was supposed to be the first Evangelion with a working S2 Engine installed, but as we know in the activation test it disappeared. Naturally, the next working engines would go with the following Evas in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Nov 11, 2009 8:26 pm They're not perfect clones. Eva-00 and Eva-02 differ from Eva-03/04, who further differ from the MP Evas, who (ironically) are the least like Adam in facial shape.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Nov 11, 2009 8:27 pm I think it's obvious that if they were trying to give the Evas an artificial S2 Engine it means they were having trouble growing the S2 organ.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Nov 11, 2009 8:29 pm Maybe they do grow one, but they remove it on purpose. SEELE clearly wasn't pleased when Unit-01 aquired one. Maybe SEELE didn't want Unit-04's artificial one to work, and they tampered with the testing somehow. Considering the influence SEELE has and that they would do anything to accomplish their goal, they would likely sacrifice an Eva and kill many people if it means their scenario is correct.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 11, 2009 8:42 pm "It's not our intention to create a new god." The Evangelions, intentionally or otherwise, are castrated from having S2 Engines at their conception, and are kept from gaining them because, well, if they went berserk, they could use them S2 Engines to erase all life on the planet. Y'know.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Nov 11, 2009 9:18 pm AuraTwilight wrote: "It's not our intention to create a new god." The Evangelions, intentionally or otherwise, are castrated from having S2 Engines at their conception, and are kept from gaining them because, well, if they went berserk, they could use them S2 Engines to erase all life on the planet. Y'know.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Wed Nov 11, 2009 9:54 pm Alternatively, the Evangelions' lack of S2 Engines sort of forces them to be reliant on cables and batteries as a source of power, which among other things, contributes to the "holy shit" factor of Yui-sama going berserk, because theoretically, an Eva without power is like a freshly-dead body stuck in a suit of armor - it's not "alive" unless it's connected to a power source, and S2 Organs/Engines are very powerful power sources, which make the Evangelions sufficiently independent of NERV to where if one goes berserk, you just can't fucking stop the thing, hence why 00, 01, 02, and 03 are made without them, and 04 exploded - SEELE doesn't want the Evangelions to be uncontrollable until they no longer have the need to control them in EoE, since they were all going to be tang anyway, and 3I was going to happen one way or another, so the MP Evas didn't need to be dependent on external power like the first four had to.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 11, 2009 10:05 pm Except Evangelions can go berserk and ignore power limitations whenever they want.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Wed Nov 11, 2009 11:17 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Except Evangelions can go berserk and ignore power limitations whenever they want.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 11, 2009 11:24 pm Except it wasn't "used up", it just got EATEN. Oh, and Unit 00, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Nov 11, 2009 11:48 pm Apart from the mysterious logistics of the cloning process, they obviously didn't WANT the Evas to have S2s since that meant they couldn't control them as reliably. The MPEs are different seeing as they seemingly perfected the DPS.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 12, 2009 12:11 am I always thought that the S2 that was installed into Unit-04 was obtained from one of the dead angels. Perhaps it was a translation that indicated that it was "restored", which begged the question from what?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Thu Nov 12, 2009 12:54 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I always thought that the S2 that was installed into Unit-04 was obtained from one of the dead angels. Perhaps it was a translation that indicated that it was "restored", which begged the question from what?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 12, 2009 12:57 am AuraTwilight wrote: Except Evangelions can ignore any rational limitations whenever they damn well please so long as it works for Anno-san's plot.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Thu Nov 12, 2009 1:00 am NemZ wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: Except Evangelions can ignore any rational limitations whenever they damn well please so long as it works for Anno-san's plot.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 12, 2009 1:19 am ^to give Maya more screen time.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Nov 12, 2009 1:20 am Vegeta 20XX wrote: This. And on that note, what's the use of remotely ejecting the Entry Plug if it always refuses to eject?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Nov 12, 2009 1:25 am Sachi wrote: Vegeta 20XX wrote: This. And on that note, what's the use of remotely ejecting the Entry Plug if it always refuses to eject?
